g.text: What 'Real' Means at Each Test Layer
Same 6-component system, viewed through four test scopes. The dashed boundary marks what's REAL; everything outside is a stub, fake, or contract-shaped peer.
As the boundary expands → more realism, more setup cost, more flake sources, harder failure localization.
Choose the smallest boundary that exercises the bug class you care about — confidence per test-second is what you are optimizing, not realism.
